# Plugins (compact)

Slim, always-loaded index of every plugin. Shows only `name ‚Äî description`; load-bearing infrastructure plugins are marked `[load-bearing]`. Read [`plugins-details.md`](./plugins-details.md) for the full reference, or open the per-plugin `CLAUDE.md` when working inside a specific plugin.

<!-- AUTOGENERATED:BEGIN ‚Äî do not edit; regenerated by `./singularity build` -->

- **`active-data`** ‚Äî Meta plugin for inline interactive widgets agents render via XML-like tags in assistant text. Sub-plugins contribute inline (pattern) or block (tag) renderers; hosts use useActiveDataSegments() + useActiveDataLinkify(). Persistent state for inline interactive widgets ‚Äî table + resource keyed by (conversationId, messageId, tag, occurrenceIndex).
  - Plugins:
    - **`attempt`** ‚Äî Renders raw `att-<id>` strings inline as clickable chips that open the attempt pane. Models emit the bare id, no tag wrapping needed.
    - **`conv`** ‚Äî Renders raw `conv-<id>` strings inline as clickable chips that open the referenced conversation in the right side pane alongside the host conversation. Models emit the bare id, no tag wrapping needed.
    - **`task`** ‚Äî Renders <task>prompt</task> tags as editable cards with Create + Launch actions. Models suggest tasks inline; users tweak and act without leaving the transcript.
    - **`task-link`** ‚Äî Renders raw `task-<id>` strings inline as clickable chips that open the task detail pane. Models emit the bare id, no tag wrapping needed.

- **`agents`** ‚Äî Named agent definitions that launch conversations. Named agent definitions that launch conversations.
  - Plugins:
    - **`auto-launch`** ‚Äî Umbrella plugin for agent auto-launch. Sub-plugins contribute row actions and settings.
      - Plugins:
        - **`toggle`** ‚Äî Toggle on/off to activate agent auto-launch. Owns the agents_ext_auto_launch side-table via the entity-extensions primitive. Server side of the agent auto-launch toggle. Owns the agents_ext_auto_launch side-table via the entity-extensions primitive.

- **`attempt-view`** ‚Äî Main pane at /a/:id showing an attempt's conversations on the left and the selected conversation on the right. Adds a toolbar button to the conversation view to switch into it.

- **`auth`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Shared authentication infrastructure (OAuth 2.0, API keys). Surfaces an Accounts sidebar entry; provider sub-plugins extend the Auth.Provider slot. Centralized OAuth/API-key infrastructure for third-party services. Tokens persist via the central secrets store; auth runs on the central runtime so all worktrees share one connected state.
  - Plugins:
    - **`google`** ‚Äî Google OAuth provider ‚Äî adds the Google row to the Accounts pane and a credentials section to Settings. Google OAuth 2.0 provider. Use with Drive, Gmail, Calendar consumer plugins via incremental scopes.
    - **`notion`** ‚Äî Notion OAuth provider (scaffold). Adds the Notion row to the Accounts pane and a credentials section to Settings. Notion OAuth provider (scaffold). Surfaces in Accounts pane; end-to-end smoke not yet validated.

- **`build`** ‚Äî Trigger `./singularity build` from the toolbar.

- **`code-explorer`** ‚Äî Worktree-scoped file browser: sidebar entry opens the main worktree; conversation toolbar opens the agent's worktree. Worktree-scoped file browser and viewer: tree listing plus raw/diff/image content by attempt id or the reserved `main` sentinel.

- **`config`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Per-worktree config. Plugins declare typed fields via defineConfig; values expose in this Settings pane. Per-worktree key/value config. Plugins declare typed fields via defineConfig; values expose in the Settings pane.

- **`conversations`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Conversation domain: shared server code and types; view plugins live under `plugins/`.
  - Plugins:
    - **`conversation-category`** ‚Äî Per-conversation category chip in the sidebar row and conversation toolbar. Auto-classified by Haiku after each turn; manual override via the toolbar chip's popover. Classifies each conversation into one of a configurable list of categories using Haiku. Surfaces the result as a chip in the sidebar row and the conversation toolbar.
    - **`conversation-progress`** ‚Äî 4-step progress bar (research ‚Üí plan ‚Üí implementation ‚Üí pushed) in the conversation toolbar and sidebar chip. Tracks each conversation through four phases (research ‚Üí design ‚Üí implementation ‚Üí pushed) via git heuristics: no files = research, only research/** = design, any other file = implementation, push event = pushed.
    - **`conversation-ui`** ‚Äî Umbrella for visual primitives that render a Conversation. Sub-plugins ship the actual components (item rows/chips, future cards/mentions/etc.).
      - Plugins:
        - **`item`** ‚Äî Visual primitive for rendering a Conversation as a row or inline chip. Used by every surface that lists conversations.
    - **`conversation-view`** ‚Äî Conversation pane host. Toolbar/title go through PaneChrome via `conversationPane.Actions`; only `Conversation.PromptBar` lives here.
      - Plugins:
        - **`action-bar`** ‚Äî Hosts the Conversation.ActionBar slot ‚Äî action buttons rendered in the JSONL viewer header.
        - **`allow-monitor`** ‚Äî Flags when an agent has created an allow-file (.allow-main, .allow-migrations) to bypass security guards.
        - **`code`** ‚Äî Meta plugin hosting code-related contributions for a conversation (edited files, viewer, etc.). Tracks edited files in the conversation's worktree via the live-state primitive.
          - Plugins:
            - **`docs-button`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that opens a sidebar listing edited markdown design docs in the conversation worktree.
            - **`file-pane`** ‚Äî Hosts the per-conversation file-peek pane and the FilePane.Renderer slot.
              - Plugins:
                - **`diff`** ‚Äî Side-by-side diff of the file vs HEAD in the conversation's worktree.
                - **`image`** ‚Äî Image preview for .png, .jpg, .gif, .webp, .svg, and similar files.
                - **`markdown`** ‚Äî Rendered markdown preview for .md and .mdx files.
                - **`raw`** ‚Äî Plain file renderer with syntax highlighting. Fallback tab for any text file.
            - **`review`** ‚Äî Toolbar button and full-screen view to review all worktree changes file-by-file. Toolbar button and full-screen view to review all worktree changes file-by-file.
        - **`commits-graph`** ‚Äî Toolbar chip showing commits ahead/behind main; opens a side pane with the chain of commits between merge-base and HEAD. Toolbar chip showing commits ahead/behind main; opens a side pane with the chain of commits between merge-base and HEAD.
        - **`drop-and-exit`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that marks the top task as dropped and closes the conversation.
        - **`exit`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that closes the conversation without changing any task state.
        - **`fork-conversation`** ‚Äî Toolbar buttons (+Sonnet / +Opus) that spin up a new conversation in the same worktree.
        - **`fork-session`** ‚Äî Toolbar buttons (+Sonnet / +Opus) that fork the current conversation via `claude --resume <id> --fork-session`.
        - **`hold-and-exit`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that marks the task as held and closes the conversation.
        - **`jsonl-viewer`** ‚Äî Renders the raw Claude JSONL session log as the conversation's main content. Hosts the JsonlViewer.EventRenderer slot for child plugins to render specific event kinds. Parses Claude's raw JSONL session log and streams it as structured events via the jsonl-events resource.
          - Plugins:
            - **`assistant-text`** ‚Äî Renders assistant text events in the JSONL viewer, with optional markdown rendering.
            - **`assistant-thinking`** ‚Äî Renders assistant thinking blocks in the JSONL viewer as collapsible sections.
            - **`assistant-tool-use`** ‚Äî Renders assistant tool-use events in the JSONL viewer.
            - **`summary`** ‚Äî Renders summary separator events in the JSONL viewer.
            - **`system`** ‚Äî Renders system events in the JSONL viewer.
            - **`user-image`** ‚Äî Renders inline image thumbnails for user-image events.
            - **`user-text`** ‚Äî Renders user text events in the JSONL viewer.
            - **`user-tool-result`** ‚Äî Renders user tool-result events in the JSONL viewer.
        - **`launch-prompts`** ‚Äî Pre-configured prompts that launch a new background conversation in the same worktree. Pre-configured prompts that launch a new background conversation in the same worktree.
        - **`model`** ‚Äî Displays the conversation model as a colored chip in the toolbar.
        - **`new-child-task`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that opens a popover to create a child task under the conversation's parent task.
        - **`open-app`** ‚Äî Opens the conversation's namespace at `http://<id>.localhost:9000/`.
        - **`prompt-input`** ‚Äî Free-form text input at the bottom of the conversation view. Enter sends a turn; fork buttons reuse the draft as the new conversation's initial prompt.
        - **`push-and-exit`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that asks Claude to push the branch and close the conversation; surfaces Claude's flag if it has anything to raise.
        - **`push-counter`** ‚Äî Displays the number of pushes for the conversation's attempt in the toolbar.
        - **`quick-prompts`** ‚Äî Named prompt chips in the conversation floating bar. Click to send a preset message to the active conversation. Named prompts that appear as chips in the conversation toolbar. Click to send a preset message.
        - **`resume`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that resumes a gone conversation via `claude --resume <claude-id>`.
        - **`side-conversation`** ‚Äî Right side pane that shows a second conversation alongside the host (read-only viewer; expand to pop out).
        - **`side-task`** ‚Äî Right side pane that shows a single task's detail alongside the host conversation (read-only-ish; expand to pop out).
        - **`status`** ‚Äî Displays the conversation status as a colored badge in the toolbar.
        - **`tasks-panel`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that opens a right pane showing the task tree (active task + children) and the task detail.
        - **`terminal-pane`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that opens a right pane attaching to the conversation's tmux session.
        - **`turn-summary`** ‚Äî Inline card above the prompt input showing a Haiku-generated summary of the latest assistant turn, with caveats and suggested actions. After every assistant turn, runs Haiku on the (user, assistant) pair to produce a one-line summary, caveats list, and actions list. Renders above the prompt input.
        - **`vscode`** ‚Äî Opens the conversation's worktree in VSCode.
    - **`conversations-view`** ‚Äî Sidebar list of all conversations.
      - Plugins:
        - **`grouped`** ‚Äî User-defined groups in the conversation sidebar list ‚Äî drag a conversation onto another to create a group; drag onto a group to join. User-defined groups in the conversation sidebar list ‚Äî drag a conversation onto another to create a group; drag onto a group to join.
        - **`history`** ‚Äî All conversations in historical order of creation.
        - **`queue`** ‚Äî Anki-style global priority queue of conversations awaiting user input. Top of the deck is what to do next; finishing a turn returns the conversation to position 2 so the top stays stable. Server side of the global Anki-style conversations queue. Owns the conversations_ext_queue side-table via the entity-extensions primitive and seeds rank on conversationCreated + conversationTurnCompleted.
    - **`model-provider`** ‚Äî Registry mapping logical ConversationModel IDs to pinned Claude CLI flags and display metadata. Registry mapping logical ConversationModel IDs to pinned Claude CLI flags and display metadata.
    - **`runtime-api`** ‚Äî Stub placeholder for running Claude via the Anthropic Agent SDK (not yet implemented).
    - **`runtime-tmux`** ‚Äî Runs Claude CLI sessions inside tmux panes.
    - **`summary`** ‚Äî Toolbar button that opens a side pane with the Summarise action and the latest structured Sonnet summary (phase, flags, next action). On-demand structured summaries of conversations: phase, flags, next action. Curated by Sonnet via MCP. Append-only history.
    - **`transcript-api`** ‚Äî Agent API: GET /api/conversations/:id/transcript returns the on-disk JSONL path for a conversation's full raw Claude session transcript.

- **`conversations-recover`** ‚Äî Sidebar entry + pane listing recently-closed conversations with restore buttons. Batch-restore recently-closed conversations that were killed by a crash.

- **`crashes`** ‚Äî Reports uncaught browser errors to the server. Records server/frontend crashes and files deduped tasks.
  - Plugins:
    - **`launch-fix`** ‚Äî Adds a Fix button to the plugin crash banner that launches an agent on the auto-created crash task with optional freeform context.

- **`debug`** ‚Äî Debug tools sidebar group.
  - Plugins:
    - **`claude-cli-calls`** ‚Äî Debug pane listing every single-shot `claude --print` call (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) with prompt, output, source, and duration.
    - **`db-backup`** ‚Äî Backup non-worktree Postgres databases to ~/.backups/singularity/. Backup non-worktree Postgres databases to ~/.backups/singularity/.
    - **`logs`** ‚Äî System logs pane, opened from the Debug sidebar.
    - **`memory`** ‚Äî Browse Claude Code auto-memory files for the current project. Browse Claude Code auto-memory files for the current project.
    - **`queue`** ‚Äî Inspect and debug the jobs queue, events emission log, and active triggers.
    - **`worktree-cleanup`** ‚Äî Audit and remove stale git worktrees and their Postgres DB forks. Audit and remove stale git worktrees and their Postgres DB forks.

- **`events-test`** ‚Äî Dummy UI for exercising the events plugin end-to-end. Dummy plugin exercising the events and jobs APIs end-to-end.

- **`health`** ‚Äî Surfaces server restarts as a toast; exposes /api/health helpers. Liveness endpoint used by clients to detect server restarts.

- **`improve`**

- **`infra`** ‚Äî Umbrella for cross-cutting server-side primitives used by feature plugins: jobs, events, secrets, mcp, attachments.
  - Plugins:
    - **`attachments`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Polymorphic file attachments. Exposes uploadAttachment() helper; storage/serve on the server plugin. Attachments on disk (UUID-named under ~/.singularity/attachments/). Consumers declare ownership with Attachments.defineLink(ownerTable); orphan sweep reclaims unreferenced rows past TTL.
    - **`claude-cli`** ‚Äî One-shot Claude CLI helper (`claude --print`) for short, latency-tolerant generations. Reuses the user's local Claude CLI auth ‚Äî no API key plumbing.
    - **`database`**
    - **`entity-extensions`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Lets sub-plugins attach typed DB fields to a parent's entity table via 1:1 side-tables. Each consumer owns its <parent>_ext_<name> table; FK CASCADE on parent delete.
    - **`events`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Event‚Üíjob bindings layered on @plugins/jobs. Plugins declare events with typed filter columns via defineTriggerEvent, subscribers bind jobs via trigger().
    - **`git-watcher`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Watches local git refs (refs/heads/main by default) via @parcel/watcher. Emits the git.refAdvanced trigger event and notifies the refHeadResource live-state resource on every advance.
    - **`jobs`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Durable background jobs primitive built on graphile-worker. Plugins declare jobs via defineJob and enqueue via job.enqueue.
    - **`mcp`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî HTTP MCP server endpoint. Hosts tools contributed by other plugins via Mcp.tool.
    - **`paths`**
    - **`secrets`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Encrypted key-value primitive. AES-256-GCM blob at ~/.singularity/secrets.json.enc with the master key in the OS keychain (fallback to ~/.singularity/secrets/.key). Hosted on the central runtime; consumers (auth, config) call /api/secrets/* via the gateway.
    - **`worktree`**

- **`miller`** ‚Äî Miller-columns layout renderer. Maps the matched pane chain to a horizontal sequence of resizable, collapsible columns.

- **`primitives`** ‚Äî Umbrella for cross-cutting client-side primitives used by feature plugins: pane router, tree, live state, networking, editable fields, syntax highlighting, launch buttons.
  - Plugins:
    - **`auto-scroll`** ‚Äî Stick-to-bottom scroll primitive for streaming surfaces. Hook tracks pin state and detects content growth via ResizeObserver; companion JumpToBottomButton offers an affordance when the user has scrolled up.
    - **`avatar`** ‚Äî Reusable circular avatar (icon + color) with an optional status-dot overlay and a chooser popover.
    - **`editable-field`** ‚Äî Debounced-autosave field hook with focus tracking, flush-on-blur, and self-echo suppression. Used by task/agent detail forms.
    - **`error-boundary`** ‚Äî Generic React error boundary primitive. Wraps plugin contributions so render errors are contained to one slot, with an ErrorBoundary.Action slot for domain-specific buttons (e.g. crash 'Fix') and a registerBoundaryReporter() hook for opt-in crash reporting.
    - **`file-links`** ‚Äî Parses inline file paths (e.g. `research/foo.md`) in plain text and renders them as clickable buttons that fire onFileOpen. Exposes <FileLinkText/>, parseFileLinks(), and linkifyChildren() for use inside ReactMarkdown component overrides.
    - **`launch`** ‚Äî Reusable Sonnet/Opus launch buttons for creating conversations.
    - **`live-state`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Server live-state primitive: useResource hook + NotificationsProvider + NotificationsClient. Thin TanStack Query wrapper over the app's leader-elected /ws/notifications channel.
    - **`networking`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî WebSocket / EventSource / fetch primitives with reconnection, status-bus, and retry. Used by live-state internally and by terminal/logs/health/stats directly.
    - **`pane`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Unified pane primitive: Pane.define, <Outlet/>, <PaneRouter/>, and chrome components.
    - **`paste-images`** ‚Äî Lexical-based prompt editor with paste-image support and rich thumbnails (hover-√ó remove, click-to-expand lightbox). Pasted images upload to the attachments primitive; editor serializes to markdown with `![](/api/attachments/<id>)` refs.
    - **`persistent-draft`** ‚Äî Generic localStorage-backed useState drop-in with optional entity scope and TTL auto-expiry. All useDraft calls sharing the same key stay in sync within and across tabs.
    - **`rank`** ‚Äî Fractional-indexing rank primitive. THE authoritative source for sortable rank strings ‚Äî use nextRankIn()/nextRankUnder() from the server barrel for new insertions; use computeDrop() from the tree plugin for DnD moves. Never use floats or integers. Fractional-indexing rank primitive. THE authoritative source for sortable rank strings. Use nextRankIn() for flat tables, nextRankUnder() for parent-scoped lists. Re-exports rankText column type. Never use floats or integers for ordering.
    - **`relative-time`** ‚Äî Formats a Date as a human-readable relative string (just now, Nm ago, Nh ago, Nd ago). Exposes formatRelativeTime() and <RelativeTime date={‚Ä¶} />.
    - **`syntax-highlight`** ‚Äî Shared shiki-based syntax highlighter primitive. Exposes getHighlighter, themeForMode, languageForPath, useDarkMode, and a <HighlightedCode> component for plugins rendering code.
    - **`tree`** ‚Äî Tree hierarchy utilities (buildTree, isDescendant, computeDrop) and a generic TreeList with composable row primitives (RowChrome, RenameInput, useTreeRow) for list plugins.

- **`publish`** ‚Äî Review the worktree's plugin tree before publishing to the marketplace. Read-only review surface for the marketplace publish flow. Walks the worktree's plugin tree and exposes it as a flat tree.

- **`reorder`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Generic reorder primitive. Slot owners opt in via Reorder.area; hosts render with Reorder.useArea. Generic reorder primitive: per-worktree storage of slot contribution ranks.
  - Plugins:
    - **`edit-mode`** ‚Äî Pen button on the top toolbar that toggles global edit mode for all reorderable slots; Esc exits edit mode.

- **`screenshot`** ‚Äî Capture the current page and edit it (crop, draw) in a new tab. Bottom prompt form launches a conversation with the edited screenshot attached. Stores in-flight screenshots so a freshly opened tab can fetch them.
  - Plugins:
    - **`draw-canvas`** ‚Äî Reusable freehand draw canvas (color/width strokes). Used by the screenshot editor and draw-on-app.
    - **`draw-on-app`** ‚Äî Toolbar button to draw freehand on the live app, capture as a screenshot with strokes baked in, and pre-attach to +improve.

- **`shell`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Foundational app layout; defines the slots and commands most other plugins extend.

- **`stats`** ‚Äî Root plugin hosting stacked chart contributions from child plugins.
  - Plugins:
    - **`commits`** ‚Äî Commit-based stats: commits and lines of change over time. Commit-based stats: commits and lines of change over time.
    - **`cost`** ‚Äî Token usage and dollar cost across Claude Code sessions, with per-conversation breakdown. Token usage and dollar cost across Claude Code sessions, sourced from ccusage.
    - **`tasks`** ‚Äî Task-based stats: active (open) tasks over time.

- **`tasks`** ‚Äî Nested tasks with attempts linking to conversations.
  - Plugins:
    - **`auto-start`** ‚Äî Owns the tasks_ext_auto_start side-table via the entity-extensions primitive. Owns the tasks_ext_auto_start side-table via the entity-extensions primitive. CAS mutations for setTaskAutoStart/claimAutoStart.
    - **`task-attachments`** ‚Äî Renders the task's attachments (images, files) in the detail pane.
    - **`task-dependencies`** ‚Äî Lists the task's dependencies as removable chips, with a quick-add button for the parent task when applicable.
    - **`task-description`** ‚Äî Description editor section in the task detail pane. Inline file-link parsing routes clicks to the active file-peek context.
    - **`task-detail`** ‚Äî Owns the /tasks pane host and the right-pane detail view for a selected task. Defines TaskDetail.{Above,Section} slots and the file-open + flush-registry contexts that section sub-plugins share.
    - **`task-draft-form`** ‚Äî Reusable popover + chain form for drafting one or more tasks. Powers the Improve toolbar button and the conversation new-child-task button.
    - **`task-events`** ‚Äî Lists pushes, attempts, and conversations for a task. Clicking a conversation opens taskConversationPane.
    - **`task-file-peek`** ‚Äî Right-panel preview for files referenced from a task description. Opens as a child pane (Miller column) of taskDetailPane.
    - **`task-graph`** ‚Äî Renders the dependency-DAG band above a task's detail when the task has dependents or dependencies.
    - **`task-header`** ‚Äî Top section of the task detail pane: editable title, status chip, hold/drop buttons, author, auto-start, and Launch buttons.
    - **`task-list`** ‚Äî Tree view of all tasks rendered in the Tasks pane. Defines Tasks.List/TaskActions slots and ships the row actions (delete, expand-all, launch-agent).
    - **`task-status`** ‚Äî Single source of truth for TaskStatus display metadata ‚Äî icon, label, icon color, and badge style.
    - **`task-title`** ‚Äî Haiku-backed task title generation. Synthesises an instant first-line fallback; upgrades asynchronously so task creation never blocks on the Claude CLI round-trip.

- **`tasks-core`** [load-bearing] ‚Äî Schema + repository layer for the tasks/attempts/conversations FK cluster.

- **`terminal`** ‚Äî Exposes view factories for terminal panes; no web contributions yet.

- **`theme`** ‚Äî Toolbar toggles for light/dark mode and a distinct theme on non-main worktrees.

- **`welcome`** ‚Äî Landing pane shown at `/`.

- **`worktree-switcher`** ‚Äî Toolbar dropdown to switch the active worktree namespace.

<!-- AUTOGENERATED:END -->
